Overview & Niche: Seven-piece from Little Rock, AR. "Psychedelic afro-futurism" — Soul, Afro-Beat, Blues, Dub, and Indie Rock. Debut album All The World There Is (2014); EP Ivory Loving Glass (2018). SXSW and Newport Folk Festival appearances. Paste Magazine "12 Arkansas Bands You Should Be Listening to Now."
USA Touring: Yes — national touring; no active dates currently
Most Recent Release: EP: Ivory Loving Glass (2018). No new release since.

Overview & Niche: German-based singer-songwriter with 11+ releases on Bandcamp. Eclectic: folk, indie, blues touches. Not a traditional blues artist — engine likely surfaces him on the folk-roots axis. Prolific studio artist with a European following.

Category: Acoustic Blues / Delta / Piedmont / Texas Blues
Overview & Niche: Georgia-born Grammy-nominated multi-style acoustic blues guitarist. Called "Wunderkind" by Taj Mahal; Keb' Mo' protégé. IBC Best Self-Produced CD (2018). Signed to Strolling Bones Records. Self-ambassador mission: reviving traditional blues for new audiences. Delta, Piedmont, Texas, and Gospel styles.
USA Touring: Yes — actively touring USA and internationally; 12+ upcoming dates
Most Recent Release: West Georgia Blues (Aug 2024, Strolling Bones Records)

Overview & Niche: New York-born, Stockholm-based folk-blues troubadour. Son of Leon Bibb; godfather Paul Robeson; grew up with Dylan and Pete Seeger visiting. 40+ albums over 5 decades. Three Grammy nominations. Multiple Blues Foundation awards. Acoustic country blues with contemporary social consciousness. Collaborators: Taj Mahal, Mavis Staples, Charlie Musselwhite. New album One Mississippi Jan 2026 via Repute Records.
USA Touring: Yes — tours USA, UK, Europe, Australia; European-based but regular USA dates
Most Recent Release: One Mississippi (Jan 2026, Repute Records) — just released

Overview & Niche: Oakland-born country blues guitarist. Mississippi country blues roots. Grammy-nominated (2003). 1996 Handy Award Best New Blues Artist. "The boy has got thunder in his hands" — Taj Mahal. Wide range: country blues, 60s/70s guitar rock, western swing. Praised by Dylan, Clapton, Mick Taylor. Has Muscle Theory electric band plus solo acoustic. Known as a "musician's musician."
USA Touring: Yes — sporadic USA dates; 2025 Denver and Nashville shows; currently UK touring
Most Recent Release: No recent studio album identified; last known release mid-2010s

Overview & Niche: Brooklyn seven-piece soul-rock band led by vocalist Arleigh Kincheloe with brother Jackson on harmonica. Bonnaroo, Firefly, Mountain Jam, Austin City Limits, Jazz & Heritage Festival appearances. Opened for Dr. John, Sharon Jones & The Dap-Kings. New album in progress per 2025 fan reports of new songs at live shows. Active USA touring.
USA Touring: Yes — actively touring USA; 2026 Fairfield Hills Music Festival booked
Most Recent Release: Fowl Play (most recent studio); new album in progress

Overview & Niche: Brazilian harmonica player and composer based in USA (Nashville). NOTE: primarily a harmonica player, not guitarist — engine surfaced her on the blues axis. Part of blues duo with guitarist Tiago Juk and band Milk'n Blues. Collaborations with Jeff Beck, Buddy Guy, Sugar Blue, Billy Branch. 342K Instagram followers. "Improvisation in C Minor" YouTube video went viral. As of 2024 touring with Dave Stewart's Eurythmics Songbook band.
USA Touring: Yes — USA-based, performs nationally and internationally
Most Recent Release: No standalone album identified; primarily releases via collaborations and YouTube

Overview & Niche: English blues-rock guitarist from Liverpool, born 1992. Multiple British Blues Awards including Young Artist of the Year three consecutive years and Guitarist of the Year. #1 UK Jazz & Blues chart with Bad Luck & The Blues (2023). Voted top 10 blues-rock guitarists worldwide by Music Radar. Battling Crohn's disease; pivoting to solo acoustic. New album On My Own (Jan 2026) on his own Ron Records. NOTE: primarily UK/Europe artist; limited USA touring history.
USA Touring: Minimal — primarily UK/Europe. Has played Carnegie Hall NYC.
Most Recent Release: On My Own (Jan 2026, Ron Records) — just released

Overview & Niche: English guitarist from Bradford/Yorkshire. First-class Honours from Leeds College of Music (100% pass mark — first in college history). Multiple British Blues Awards. Toured with Joe Bonamassa. Independent on own Tis Rock Music label. Most recent album The Healing (May 2025) is explicitly prog-rock/heavy rock — has moved significantly away from blues. NOTE: UK/Europe artist; limited USA touring.

Los Angeles-born electric blues guitarist and singer. Professional since early 1980s. Guitarist for Albert Collins & The Icebreakers (1988–91). Also played with Maggie Mayall & The Cadillacs and Fingers Taylor & Ladyfinger Revue. 13 solo albums since 1993. Two Blues Music Awards (1997, 2010). Pioneer for women in electric blues guitar. Collaborators: Tab Benoit, Coco Montoya, Charlie Musselwhite, Peter Green, Mick Taylor, Duke Robillard, Tommy Shannon (Double Trouble). Based in USA; continues to record and tour.
Most Recent Release: Holdin' Court (2009, Vizz Tone) — most recent studio album identified; may have later releases

Overview & Niche: Los Angeles-based blues/soul/Americana vocalist. Grammy-nominated. BB King Entertainer of the Year 2009 (only second woman after Koko Taylor). Seven Blues Music Awards. 17 albums. Known for powerful interpretive style. Long partnership with producer Dave Darling. Alligator Records alumna. Latest album Back for Me (March 2025, Blue Élan Records) features Joe Bonamassa and Sue Foley. Actively touring USA.
Most Recent Release: Back for Me (March 2025, Blue Élan Records)

Overview & Niche: Shreveport-born Texas blues-rock guitarist. Professional since age 14. Toured with Johnnie Taylor (Chitlin Circuit), Lucky Peterson (worldwide), Buddy Miles (mentor/co-producer). Close friendship with Johnny Winter (taught slide guitar) and Billy Gibbons. Jeff Beck: "a very exciting and intense blues guitarist." Part of supergroup Supersonic Blues Machine. European Comeback Tour Fall 2024. New band Two Wolf (blues-rock/southern rock) debut album June 2025 via Cleopatra Records. Nashville-based. Active touring USA and Europe.
USA Touring: Yes — actively touring USA and Europe
Most Recent Release: Two Wolf debut (June 2025, Cleopatra Records); live album recorded Montreux 2025 pending release

Overview & Niche: Ottawa-born Canadian multi-instrumentalist (upright bass, harmonica, guitar, piano, cigar box guitar) and vocalist. Family band: father (drums) and sisters (sax, trombone, trumpet). 2023 JUNO Award winner Blues Album of the Year for Long River. 2025 album Not Defeated nominated for eight 2026 Canadian Blues Music Awards; features Kingfish and Eric Gales. Opened for Buddy Guy, Gary Clark Jr., Keb' Mo', Joe Bonamassa, Eric Gales. Folk Canada 2024 Performer of the Year. Philadelphia Folkfest, Montreal Jazz Festival, Ottawa BluesFest appearances. American TV debut on Oprah's OWN network as a child.
USA Touring: Yes — tours Canada and USA; 2026 dates in progress

Overview & Niche: 25-year-old Indigenous Canadian guitarist and vocalist from Alert Bay, BC. Gravel-soaked baritone; AC/DC-meets-Texas-blues guitar style. Debut Same Pain (2023) — Top 20 UK Blues Albums chart, Top 20 US Roots Music Blues Rock chart, BBC Radio 2 airplay, two Western Canadian Music Award nominations. Second album Bill's Café (Feb 2026) produced by Grammy-winner Tom Hambridge (Buddy Guy, Kingfish, Susan Tedeschi); Gulf Coast Records/Mike Zito. Opened for Kenny Wayne Shepherd, John Fogerty, Kingfish, Joe Bonamassa. Joe Bonamassa Blues Cruise performer. Exclaim! 2026 Most Anticipated Albums. Kingfish connection via producer and touring is notable.
USA Touring: Yes — Canada and USA touring; 2026 tour underway

Overview & Niche: Australian four-piece blues-soul band from Melbourne. Powerhouse vocalist Josh Teskey fronts a full-band sound rooted in soul, gospel, and blues-rock. Critically acclaimed; multiple ARIA Award wins. Albums include Run Home Slow (2019) and The Winding Way (2023). Known for extraordinary live performances. Active international touring including USA.
USA Touring: Yes — actively touring USA; festival appearances

Overview & Niche: Atlanta-based blues, soul, and R&B vocalist and multi-instrumentalist. Retro-soul approach with deep blues roots; independently released and self-produced. Rising star on the blues circuit. Multiple festival bookings including Big Blues Bender. Actively touring USA.

Boston traditional blues trio: Matt Stubbs (guitar), Cody Nilsen (guitar/vocals), Josh Kiggans (drums). Mission-driven: bringing raw traditional electric blues forward into a modern context — Chicago, Delta, and early R&B. Recorded with special guests including Sugaray Rayford and Don Covay. On Karma Chief / Colemine Records. Active USA touring.

Los Angeles blues-rock trio. Raw, stripped-down blues-rock — slide guitar, upright bass, drums. Self-released debut All Of This Life (2016) went Top 10 on Americana chart. Atlantic Records deal followed. Known for energetic live shows. Slower release cadence recently but actively touring.

Tennessee two-piece blues-rock duo: Chris Tapp (vocals/guitar) and Brian Mullins (drums). Raw, loud, and minimal — a power-duo in the Jack White/Black Keys tradition. Relentlessly touring artists. Multiple albums on Mascot Records. Strong live reputation.

Canadian guitarist, songwriter, and producer from Winnipeg. Slide guitar and lap steel specialist. Member of The Bros. Landreth. Solo career building momentum. Warm, melodic approach — blues, roots, and indie sensibilities. Active USA and Canadian touring. Produced by Aaron Comess (Spin Doctors).

Signal: BMA 2025 winner — Contemporary Blues Male + Album
Overview & Niche: Chicago blues guitarist and vocalist. Son of Lonnie Brooks. Grammy-nominated. Multiple BMA wins in 2025 including Contemporary Blues Album winner for Blues In My DNA. Deep Chicago blues tradition with contemporary energy. Active USA touring.

BMA 2025 Best Emerging Artist winner. Contemporary blues band. Limited online presence identified — worth investigating further. Signal: BMA nomination process is highly curated; emerging artist winners represent strong practitioner consensus.
Louisiana-born singer-songwriter and guitarist. Contemporary blues and soul with strong Southern roots. BMA Best Emerging Artist nominee 2025. Active USA touring.

Signal: BMA 2025 Traditional Blues Male nominee + Album nominee
Overview & Niche: Mississippi Hill Country blues guitarist and drummer. Grandson of R.L. Burnside. Grammy-nominated (2021). Preserves and evolves the hypnotic Hill Country blues tradition. Multiple BMA nominations. Active USA touring.

Signal: BMA 2025 Acoustic Album nominee / KTBA Alaska 2025
Overview & Niche: New York-based multi-instrumentalist — guitar, banjo, fiddle, piano, harmonica. Specializes in pre-war blues, ragtime, gospel, and jazz. Known as "Blind Boy Paxton." Extraordinary range and historical depth. BMA Acoustic Album nominee 2025 for Things Done Changed. Featured on KTBA Alaska 2025. Active USA touring.
Most Recent Release: Things Done Changed (2024, BMA Acoustic Album nominee)

Signal: BMA 2025 Contemporary Blues Female nominee / KTBA XI 2026 headliner
Overview & Niche: Los Angeles blues-rock and soul vocalist. Powerhouse singer with multiple decades of recording and touring. Grammy-nominated. Collaborations with Joe Bonamassa (Don't Explain, 2011). Major international touring artist. Multiple BMA nominations over career. NOTE: High-profile artist with large following — may already be on your radar.
Most Recent Release: A Tribute to Led Zeppelin (2022); You Still Got Me (2024)

Signal: KTBA XI 2026 / Playing local — Rams Head On Stage Apr 29 2026 (passed; preferred seats gone, weeknight, pricier than usual — watch for return visit)
Overview & Niche: Indiana three-piece led by guitarist/vocalist Josh "Rev" Peyton. Raw, high-energy country blues — slide guitar, washboard, drums. Self-described "dirt blues." Active touring machine; over 200 shows per year historically. Fiercely independent. Multiple albums on Side One Dummy Records. Known for intense DIY live presence.
Most Recent Release: Dance Songs For Hard Times (2021); Father, Son & The Dirty Midnights (upcoming)

Overview & Niche: Wisconsin-born blues-rock guitarist based in Nashville. Thumbpick player influenced by Rory Gallagher and Stevie Ray Vaughan. Multiple European festival appearances; strong UK blues-rock following. Active USA touring. Known for raw, live-feel recordings. Signed to Mascot/Provogue Records.

Italian-British brothers (Marco and Max Cinelli) based in London. Blues-rock and vintage R&B. Extensive touring across Europe and USA including festival circuit appearances. Known for tight ensemble playing and old-school roots sound. Multiple albums.

Ohio-born acoustic country blues guitarist and singer. Deep pre-war blues and roots influences — Delta, Piedmont, country. Toured with Reverend Peyton's Big Damn Band. Rising artist in acoustic blues; praised by American Blues Scene and Living Blues. Young artist with strong traditional roots credentials.

Dutch blues-rock trio. Organ-driven vintage sound — draws from 1960s/70s blues-rock, gospel, and psychedelia. Major festival act in Europe; growing USA presence. Prolific: 10+ albums. Signed to Continental Records. Pablo van de Poel is a standout guitarist. NOTE: primarily European-based but KTBA booking signals USA push.
USA Touring: Growing — primarily European; KTBA 2027 signals expanding USA dates
